codeReview.summary: Re-review fails: four prior blocking findings remain unresolved in current repository state (path traversal, pi-mode wiring, unsafe String.to_atom on route params, and frontend error-body propagation). Previously fixed regressions for worker lifecycle, extract catch-all handling, and pipeline nil completed_steps handling are still present.

issues.description: Writer still writes `Path.join(staging_dir, rel_path)` without validating that `rel_path` stays under the intended output subtree. Inputs like `../` (or absolute paths) can escape the target directory; no Path.expand prefix guard is implemented.

Executor still only routes `:llm` through real codegen and sends all other modes to simulated execution. Because `/api/build` maps `mode=pi` to `:pi` (`lib/bropilot/api/handlers/work.ex:80`), pi-mode builds can return simulated success instead of dispatching real Task.Agent codegen.
GET map-slot handler still converts untrusted `slot` route params using `String.to_atom/1`, allowing unbounded atom creation from external input.
PUT map-slot handler repeats the same `String.to_atom/1` conversion on user-controlled route params, preserving the atom exhaustion crash vector.

Frontend API helpers still discard non-2xx server error bodies (`fetchApi` returns null, and `clientPost/clientGet` throw generic `Server responded <status>` at lines 272/284). Required backend messages (e.g., missing `.bropilot`, domain worker not started) are not surfaced to callers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No worker transcript skeleton or handoff exists for this fix feature, so review had to be performed against raw repository state rather than a feature-scoped implementation record.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json entry for `fix-scrutiny-m1-security-and-wiring` has `workerSessionIds: []` and `completedWorkerSessionId: null` (jq query), and no matching handoff file exists under mission `handoffs/`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md still lacks an explicit atom-safety rule forbidding `String.to_atom/1` on user-controlled input, and the same unsafe pattern remains in route handlers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md coding conventions do not mention atom safety; `lib/bropilot/api/handlers/project.ex:128` and `:149` still call `String.to_atom(slot_str)` on URL params.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture knowledge remains stale about web surface and API size.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md:39` still claims 34 REST endpoints and line 69 still says domain/Act 2 page is missing, while `web/src/pages/domain.astro` exists.

summary: FAIL. Re-run verification shows the four original blocking security/wiring findings are still present in current code. Previously fixed regressions (unlinked worker starts, extract-state catch-alls, and pipeline nil completed_steps handling) remain fixed, but unresolved blockers prevent pass.
